Patch Set 5:

Sigh, it looks like I would need to make some more major changes to the headers to have MAX_OBD_DEVICES available here, as we had previously tried to isolate that constant to allow dynamic device limits at some point.  It looks like it would be possible to split the lu_tgt_* structs/constants/helpers from lu_object.h into a separate header, but that is larger than what I want to do here, and Neil is already doing some reorg in this area in https://review.whamcloud.com/41768 "LU-8837 target: don't build any 'target' on client".

Interestingly, when I reverted the patch back to the previous version, it re-added James' and John's Code-review and the Maloo/Jenkins Verified tags.

parents.subject: LU-14588 o2ib: make config script aware of the ofed symbols

commit.subject: LU-14055 lmv: reduce struct lmv_obd size
commit.message:
LU-14055 lmv: reduce struct lmv_obd size

The lmv_obd struct contains lmv_mdt_descs which is large enough
to reference 512 * 512 = 262144 targets, but there can be only
65536 OSTs or MDTs in a single filesystem today.

Shrink the allocation size to match the current limits, reducing
the size of obd_device.u since this is the largest union member.

This reduces the size of each obd_device from 6752 to 4568 bytes.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I752b021bdb5d02e3ead3bb266121be5dbf3ebbe5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41162
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <jhammond@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
